dialogueChunks.intro: The situation has changed, {player_name}. And not for the better.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} have split the hostages into two groups and moved them to separate locations. A runner spotted one group being taken deeper {location_hint}, while the other is still at the original site. {target_npc}'s message says they're being forced to harvest {quest_item} for the {enemy_type}: those who work eat, those who refuse don't. We need to deal with both locations, but hitting one will alert the other. You'll have to be fast and brutal.
dialogueChunks.outro: Two locations, limited time, and they'll kill the hostages the moment they suspect a rescue. The pressure just doubled.

playerResponses.text: Split groups means I need to hit hard and fast. Give me the locations.
Two sites, one rescuer? The odds aren't great, but the hostages don't have better options.
Splitting the hostages means they're prepared for a rescue. This is a trap.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, they took another one. While you were tracking the first group, they circled back.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The blacksmith's apprentice is gone: snatched while locking up the forge last night. The {enemy_type} are sending a message: they can reach us whenever they want. {target_npc} found a note scratched into the forge door: coordinates leading {location_hint} and a crude drawing of what looks like a cage. Either they're taunting us or it's a genuine clue. We need you to investigate and gather {quest_item} from the area: the forge needs materials to equip the militia we're forming to guard the settlement.
dialogueChunks.outro: They're hunting us in our own home. We need to turn the tables before there's no one left to take.

playerResponses.text: A taunting message means they're overconfident. I'll use that against them.
More abductions while I'm out searching? We need to solve this at the source.
They can grab people from inside the settlement walls? Nothing I do will be enough.

dialogueChunks.intro: One of the hostages escaped, {player_name}. Barely alive, but they brought crucial information.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The escapee says the {enemy_type} have a network of tunnels {location_hint} connecting several holding sites. {target_npc} and the other captives are being held in the deepest chamber, behind at least three guard posts. The tunnels are rigged with crude alarms: tripwires, loose stones, anything that makes noise. A direct assault would give them time to move or kill the hostages. We need {quest_item} to create a diversion at the main entrance while you slip in through the drainage vent the escapee used.
dialogueChunks.outro: We have a way in, but it's narrow, dangerous, and leaves no room for error.

playerResponses.text: The drainage vent. A diversion at the front. And no room for error. Let's do it.
Tunnel networks and alarm systems? At least now we know what we're dealing with.
Booby-trapped tunnels and a single narrow entrance? That's a death sentence.

dialogueChunks.plotStep: We managed to gather some {quest_item} for the ransom, but it's barely half what they demanded. {target_npc} smuggled out word that the {enemy_type} are arguing among themselves: some want to execute the hostages as a warning, others want to keep them as workers. The division in their ranks might be our best chance. If you can reach the stronghold {location_hint} during the argument and strike while they're distracted, we might pull this off. But if the hardliners win the debate before you get there, it's over.
dialogueChunks.outro: An enemy divided is an enemy vulnerable. But the window is closing by the hour.

playerResponses.text: Internal division is an opportunity. I'll strike before they resolve it.
Racing against a debate among monsters. Strange days. Give me the approach route.
Gambling on an internal dispute? Too many unknowns.
